REPLY IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO COMPEL DEFT JOHN PIETRO'S CONTINUED DEPOSITION AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIUONS FILED BY PLAINTIFF WILLIAMS, MARY , AN INDIVIDUAL MCGUINNESS, MICHAEL , AN INDIVIDUAL

DISCOVERY 610, PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT JOHN PIETRO'S CONTINUED DEPOSITION AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART, DENIED IN PART, AS FOLLOWS: 1. DEFENDANT SHALL PRODUCE THE SUBJECT LOGS AND RECORDS FOR INSPECTION AND COPYING ON OR BEFORE DECEMBER 31, 2009, AT 12:00 NOON; 2. DEFENDANT MAY REDACT THE DOCUMENTS TO THE EXTENT THAT THEY REFER TO PROPERTIES OTHER THAN THAT IN ISSUE; AND/OR TENANTS OTHER THAN PLAINTIFFS AND THE NEIGHBORING TENANT, MR. FARBER; 3. THE DISCLOSURE OF THE DESCRIBED DOCUMENTS AND THEIR CONTENTS SHALL BE LIMITED TO THE PARTIES; THEIR COUNSEL; ANY EMPLOYEE OF COUNSEL TO WHOM DISCLOSURE IS NECESSARY IN FURTHERANCE OF THIS CASE; AND/OR EXPERT CONSULTED OR RETAINED BY COUNSEL. IN THE EVENT THAT INFORMATION IS DISCLOSED TO ANY SUCH EMPLOYEE, EXPERT OR CONSULTANT, THEY SHALL BE INFORMED OF THESE RESTRICTIONS, AND PROVIDED WITH A COPY OF THIS ORDER. 4. AT THE CONCLUSION OF THIS ACTION BY SETTLEMENT OR FINAL JUDGMENT, PLAINTIFFS SHALL RETURN THE DOCUMENTS, AND ANY COPIES, TO DEFENDANTS; 5. IF NECESSARY TO EXPLAIN ANY OF THE DOCUMENTS PRODUCED, PLAINTIFFS MAY RESUME THE DEPOSITION OF JOHN PIETRO, NOT TO EXCEED ONE HOUR OF ACTUAL TESTIMONY, WHICH DEPOSITION SHALL BE CONDUCTED NOT LATER THAN JANUARY 4, 2010. 6. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. COMMISSIONER: PAUL SLAVIT.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS MARY WILLIAMS AND MICHAEL MCGUINNESS DEMURRER TO DEFENDANTS' ANSWER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. RULING: NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - ALTHOUGH UNTIMELY UNDER CCP SECTION 430.40(B), THE COURT FINDS EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES 2, 4, 5, 7, 8, 9, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 18, 19 AND 20. DEFENDANTS MUST IDENTIFY WHICH CAUSES OF ACTION THE DEFENSES ARE DIRECTED TO AND SET FORTH NEW FACTS THAT DEFENDANTS BASE THEIR CLAIM ON APART FROM WHAT IS IN THE COMPLAINT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Notice Of Motion To Compel Deft John Pietro'S Continued Deposition And Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-24-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
MARY CATHERINE WIEDERHOLD, ESQ., (415)533-0735, FOR PLAINTIFF/MOVING PARTY
FULVIO A. PICERNO, ESQ., (415)982-6300, FOR DEFENDANT/OPPOSING PARTY WHO APPEARED TELEPHONICALLY
THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. Plaintiffs' Motion To Compel Defendant John Pietro's Continued Deposition And Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions is granted in part, denied in part, as follows: 1. Defendant shall produce the subject logs and records for inspection and copying on or before December 31, 2009, at 12:00 noon; 2. Defendant may redact the documents to the extent that they refer to properties other than that in issue; and/or tenants other than plaintiffs and the neighboring tenant, Mr. Farber; 3. The disclosure of the described documents and their contents shall be limited to the parties; their counsel; any employee of counsel to whom disclosure is necessary in furtherance of this case; and/or expert consulted or retained by counsel. In the event that information is disclosed to any such employee, expert or consultant, they shall be informed of these restrictions, and provided with a copy of this order. 4. At the conclusion of this action by settlement or final judgment, plaintiffs shall return the documents, and any copies, to defendants; 5. If necessary to explain any of the documents produced, plaintiffs may resume the deposition of John Pietro, not to exceed one hour of actual testimony, which deposition shall be conducted not later than January 4, 2010. 6. Request for sanctions is denied. COMMISSIONER: PAUL SLAVIT. (610/PS)
